title: BlockableAndUnblockable
lean_code_with_comments:
def IsUnblockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ∧
  1 ≤ k ∧ ∃ (vkm1 vk vkp1 : Node),
    p.vertices[k - 1]? = some vkm1 ∧
    p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧
    p.vertices[k + 1]? = some vkp1 ∧
    (p.edges[k - 1]? = some (vk, vkm1) ∧ (vk, vkm1) ∈ G.E → vkm1 ∈ G.Sc vk) ∧
    (p.edges[k]? = some (vk, vkp1) ∧ (vk, vkp1) ∈ G.E → vkp1 ∈ G.Sc vk)

def IsBlockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ∧ ¬ p.IsUnblockableNonCollider k
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsUnblockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ∧
  1 ≤ k ∧ ∃ (vkm1 vk vkp1 : Node),
    p.vertices[k - 1]? = some vkm1 ∧
    p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧
    p.vertices[k + 1]? = some vkp1 ∧
    (p.edges[k - 1]? = some (vk, vkm1) ∧ (vk, vkm1) ∈ G.E → vkm1 ∈ G.Sc vk) ∧
    (p.edges[k]? = some (vk, vkp1) ∧ (vk, vkp1) ∈ G.E → vkp1 ∈ G.Sc vk)

def IsBlockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ∧ ¬ p.IsUnblockableNonCollider k
lean_explanation:
The definition introduces two `Prop`-valued predicates on walks in a CDMG, both indexed by a natural-number position `k`. The implicit binder `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]` is the ambient vertex carrier — the LN's silent identification of $J \cup V$ with some carrier type. The namespace-level `variable {G : CDMG Node}` carries the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier ``let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG'' through every signature, while `{u v : Node}` and `p : Walk G u v` package the walk $\pi$ from $v_0 = u$ to $v_n = v$ via Mathlib-style endpoint binders. The position `k : ℕ` matches the LN's $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ enumeration.

The first predicate, `IsUnblockableNonCollider`, is the central encoding. Three groups of conjuncts realise clauses (i)–(iii) of the LN. Clause (i) is `p.IsNonCollider k`, reused verbatim from `def_3_15`. Clause (ii) — interior position $1 \le k \le n - 1$ — is split between an explicit `1 ≤ k` guard and an implicit upper bound recovered from the existential `p.vertices[k + 1]? = some vkp1` (which fails when `k + 1 > p.length`). Clause (iii) is the two-implication SC condition, one per walk-incident index $i \in \{k - 1, k\}$, with the LN's backward writing $(v_k, v_{k - 1}) \in E$ on the left slot and forward writing $(v_k, v_{k + 1}) \in E$ on the right slot.

The Lean-specific idioms are mostly Option-valued list lookups: `p.vertices[k]?` and `p.edges[k]?` evaluate to `some` exactly when `k` is in range, otherwise to `none`. Combined with `= some vkm1` / `= some (vk, vkm1)` they read off the LN's $v_{k-1}, v_k, v_{k+1}$ and the candidate walk-edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ directly, while doubling as implicit in-range witnesses. The membership `vkm1 ∈ G.Sc vk` reuses the strongly connected component operator from `def_3_5` (item~vii), defined as $\mathrm{Anc}^G(v) \cap \mathrm{Desc}^G(v)$ and already a `Set Node`-valued projection on the CDMG; the in-set notation lets the conclusion mirror the LN's $v_{k - 1} \in \mathrm{Sc}^G(v_k)$ verbatim.

The second predicate, `IsBlockableNonCollider`, has the minimal shape `p.IsNonCollider k ∧ ¬ p.IsUnblockableNonCollider k` — a non-collider that fails to be unblockable. The two implications and the existentials all live inside the negated `IsUnblockableNonCollider`; the `IsNonCollider` conjunct restricts the classifier to the non-collider sub-class.
design_choices:
**Walk-edge reading, not existential-shorthand reading.** Each implication fires on the walk's *specific* incident edge — the antecedent reads `p.edges[k - 1]? = some (vk, vkm1)`, not a generic existence claim about edges in `G`. The LN source block enumerated three pattern cases (left chain, right chain, fork) glossed as ``outgoing edges on $\pi$'', and the canonical tex's ``Reconciliation'' paragraph commits to the walk-edge reading: a directed edge $(v_k, w) \in E$ that does not appear as $a_{k-1}$ or $a_k$ along $\pi$ is irrelevant to the classification of $k$ on $\pi$. The existential reading would mis-classify any interior node that happens to have an unrelated outgoing edge in $G$.

**Two implications, one per walk-incident slot, rather than three pattern cases.** The LN visual writings (left chain / right chain / fork) are subsumed by a single slot-quantified condition spelled out as two implications. Left chain fires only the $i = k - 1$ antecedent, right chain only $i = k$, fork both. End-positions are filtered upstream by clause (ii); collider positions by the `IsNonCollider` precondition. Three disjoint pattern clauses would re-derive the slot structure three times and reintroduce the literal pattern-overlap at directed self-loops that the walk-edge unfolding resolves uniformly.

**Explicit `(vk, vkp1) ∈ G.E` conjunct inside each antecedent.** For the $i = k - 1$ slot this conjunct is redundant — the walk constraint already forces $(v_k, v_{k - 1}) \in E$. For the $i = k$ slot it is load-bearing: the walk constraint admits both $(v_k, v_{k + 1}) \in E$ and $(v_k, v_{k + 1}) \in L$ (a bidirected forward edge), and the LN restricts the unblockable condition to the directed case. Keeping both implications symmetric in shape makes the LN reading transparent.

**Asymmetric encoding: `Unblockable` positive, `Blockable` as negation.** Defining `IsBlockableNonCollider := IsNonCollider ∧ ¬ IsUnblockableNonCollider` makes the LN's mutual exclusivity (``every non-collider is exactly one of unblockable or blockable'') definitional rather than an external theorem. Encoding the disjunctive form for blockable as primary was rejected: it would duplicate the unblockable case-split with negated polarities and owe an external proof of mutual exclusivity that the negation encoding gives for free.

**The `IsNonCollider` conjunct in `Blockable` is load-bearing.** Without it, any collider position would automatically satisfy `¬ IsUnblockableNonCollider` (since `IsUnblockableNonCollider` carries `IsNonCollider` as its first clause), and every collider would be mis-classified as blockable. The LN restricts both classifications to the non-collider sub-class, and this conjunct is the predicate-level encoding of that restriction.

**Explicit `1 ≤ k` guard, implicit upper bound.** Lean's `ℕ` subtraction is truncated ($0 - 1 = 0$), so without the guard `p.vertices[k - 1]?` at $k = 0$ would mis-target the $v_{k - 1}$ slot. The upper bound is recovered through `p.vertices[k + 1]? = some vkp1`, which fails when $k + 1 > p.\mathrm{length}$. End-positions therefore fail the predicate automatically, matching the LN's interior-only scope. Reusing `G.Sc` from `def_3_5` ($v \in \mathrm{Sc}^G(v)$ is automatic) is what makes the directed self-loop case $(v_k, v_k) \in E$ satisfy the SC requirement without special-casing.
